creator: pav@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: feedback->closed

Maintainer replied: 

I put the note below in the pkg-message at the last update. 

Basically, xfce4-power-manager compiles, works partly, but the main 
functionality is broken.  With some effort, patches could probably 
be developed to make this work: either patches to the code here, or 
patches to HAL's device attribute names. 

Most of what this does can also be achieved using xscreensaver and 
FreeBSD's built-in power management. 

For the time-being, I would mark this port as BROKEN and available 
for a new maintainer.
